title: Muhlenbergia setiglumis
taxonomy: Muhlenbergia setiglumis is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Chloridoideae, which is a group of grasses that are commonly found in warm and temperate regions. The genus Muhlenbergia contains around 40 species of grasses, many of which are native to North and South America. Muhlenbergia setiglumis is native to Mexico and is found in dry, rocky areas. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Muhlenbergia setiglumis from other similar species of grass.

intro: Muhlenbergia setiglumis, also known as hairy wild three-awn grass or hairy Muhly, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Muhlenbergia setiglumis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a tea or infused in water, with the most common herbal preparations being teas, infusions, and tinctures made from the dried leaves and roots of the plant.
